page: Microsoft Azure D2alds_v6 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the Daldv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 3.7 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

Memory and storage configurations are tailored for low-memory workloads. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of RAM, yielding 2.0 GB per core. For local storage, it includes a 118 GB local disk and is Premium Storage capable. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this instance.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% for both stress-ng and Geekbench tests. However, multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing fall into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only small block cached bandwidth scoring in the bottom 10%, while large block write bandwidth is average. Database operations and Redis SET operations show weak to poor performance, and static web serving throughput is similarly limited.

Given its hardware profile, the D2alds_v6 offers a cost-efficient entry point for workloads requiring dedicated high-frequency single-core execution but minimal memory footprint. It is suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks, basic text processing, and small-scale web serving, but is not recommended for memory-intensive databases, parallelized compilation, or heavy multi-threaded computing.
description: Microsoft Azure D2alds_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Daldv6 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It features 2 vCPUs (1 physical core, 2 threads) and a low-memory configuration of 4.0 GB RAM. The instance includes 118 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng and Geekbench tests. However, multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations are weak, with database tasks scoring in the bottom 10%. This resource-constrained profile makes the instance a cost-efficient option for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic text processing, and small-scale web serving, while rendering it unsuitable for memory-intensive or highly parallelized applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2alds_v6 is a 2-vCPU virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor, 4.0 GB RAM, and 118 GB local storage, offering strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2alds_v6 is a 2-vCPU virtual machine with an AMD EPYC processor, 4.0 GB RAM, and 118 GB local storage for lightweight workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU virtual machine featuring high single-core clock speeds, low memory density, and integrated local storage for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs running at 3.7 GHz
Low-memory configuration of 4.0 GB RAM (2.0 GB per core)
118 GB local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench benchmarks
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and basic text processing
